titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Shepard Krech Collection of George Cruikshank
titlestmt.author: by Lucretia Baskin

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:24

did.unittitle: Shepard Krech collection of George Cruikshank

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of letters, original pencil sketches, drawings, etchings, and engravings, and books by English artist, caricaturist, and book illustrator George Cruikshank.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Estate of Shepard Krech, 1968.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: George Cruikshank (1792-1878)
bioghist.p: George Cruikshank was a British artist, caricaturist, and illustrator. Early in his career he was known for his satirical etchings of the royal family and politicians. In the 1820s, Cruikshank began to focus on book illustrations. He illustrated Charles Dickens's Sketches by Boz (1836) and Oliver Twist (1838), as well as W. Harrison Ainsworth's Rookwood (1836) and The Tower of London (1840).

bioghist.head: Shepard Krech (circa 1891-1968)
bioghist.p: Shepard Krech was a physician born in St. Paul, Minnesota. He graduated from Yale University in 1913. After serving in the United States Army in World War I, he studied pre-medicine at Columbia University and Harvard University. Krech graduated Harvard Medical School in 1923. He was on staff at Bellevue Hospital in New York City as an instructor in surgery but retired from active medical practice in 1939 after a coronary attack. He was elected to the New York Academy of Medicine in 1932 and also served as president of the Eastern Surgical Society. Krech died in 1968 at the age of 77 in East Hampton, New York.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Shepard Krech Collection of George Cruikshank. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: MS Vault Cruikshank, MS Vault File Cruikshank

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of letters, original pencil sketches, drawings, etchings, engravings, and books by English artist, caricaturist, and book illustrator George Cruikshank. Included is an illustrated manuscript for Cruikshank's The Political House That Jack Built , satirical works, and printed illustrations for books by Miguel de Cervantes, Charles Dickens, and others. Also present is a set of typescript and manuscript catalog cards created by Krech that document the collection's contents.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Shepard Krech Collection of George Cruikshank is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, a pictorial letter, drawings, prints, and a photograph of George Cruikshank.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume, containing two etchings with hand coloring by George Cruikshank, and Cruikshank's autograph manuscript description of the scenes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a carte-de-visite photograph of Cruikshank by H. J. Whitlock, Birmingham. Accompanied by slipcase.

did.unittitle: Autograph manuscript letter, signed, to Thomas Rodd, with portrait engraving of Cruikshank by unidentified artist

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With envelope. Likely removed from: Kane O'Hara, Tom Thumb: a burletta, altered from Henry Fielding (London : Thomas Rodd, 1830) (BEIN 1978 1338).

did.unittitle: Cruikshankiana: Political satires and miscellaneous tracts

title.part: The Political House that Jack Built

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Illustrated manuscript containing watercolor drawings. Accompanied by a printed copy of the text (49th edition, 1820), and a red morocco slipcase.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, sketches, prints, clipped signatures, printed items, and other papers relating to George Cruikshank.

did.unittitle: Envelope containing George Cruikshank's original pencil sketches and letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Napoleon's Soliloquy at Calais," other prints relating to the life of Napoleon, and a hand-colored copy of the Theatrical Atlas .

did.unittitle: 34 small etchings on 6 numbered plates. The theme is "time"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, prints, and sketches by George Cruikshank. Also included are Shepard Krech's catalog cards of his collection.

did.unittitle: 25 plates for the Tower of London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript index cards created by Krech listing printed and manuscript items in his collection of George Cruikshank.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters addressed to Henry Mayhew, Captain I. C. Rogers, Harrison Ainsworth, Robins, and others. Some of the documents also include sketches by Cruikshank. Accompanied by auction catalogue description from Parke-Bernet Galleries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some of the documents also include sketches by Cruikshank. Accompanied by auction catalogue description from Parke-Bernet Galleries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper lampshade panels featuring Cruikshank's etchings illustrating Adelbert von Chamisso's Peter Schlemihl . With hand coloring.

did.unittitle: George Cruikshank autograph manuscripts with original sketches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume. Includes "The Hatchet - 1822", "King Robert the Bruce", and "The Young Lady's Own Book".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript labels from two solander boxes that original housed the collection.
